items.title: Andes-virus cluster — MV Hondius cruise (South Atlantic), multi-country
items.summary: 11 confirmed and 2 probable/inconclusive Andes-virus cases on the MV Hondius, departed Ushuaia, Argentina on 2026-04-01 with stops in Antarctica, South Georgia Island, Tristan da Cunha, Saint Helena, Ascension Island. CDC met the ship at Las Palmas, Canary Islands on 2026-05-07. 3 deaths reported. Andes virus is the only hantavirus with documented person-to-person transmission.
items.citation: WHO Disease Outbreak News (2026-DON604). (2026). Andes-virus cluster — MV Hondius cruise (South Atlantic), multi-country. Retrieved 2026-06-12 from https://www.who.int/emergencies/disease-outbreak-news/item/2026-DON604.

items.title: WHO DON — Hantavirus cluster, cruise ship (2026-DON601)
items.summary: As of 13 May, a total of 11 cases, including three deaths, have been reported (case fatality ratio 27%). Eight cases were laboratory-confirmed for Andes virus (ANDV) infection, two are probable, and one case remains inconclusive and undergoing further testing. Through the International Health Regulations (2005) (IHR) channels, National IHR Focal Points (NFPs

items.title: WHO DON — Hantavirus cluster, cruise ship (2026-DON600)
items.summary: As of 8 May, a total of eight cases, including three deaths (case fatality ratio 38%), have been reported. Six cases have been laboratory-confirmed as hantavirus infections, with all identified as Andes virus (ANDV). Through the International Health Regulations (2005) (IHR) channel, National IHR Focal Points (NFPs) have all been informed and are supporting i

items.summary: As of 4 May 2026, seven cases (two laboratory confirmed cases of hantavirus and five suspected cases) have been identified, including three deaths, one critically ill patient and three individuals reporting mild symptoms. Illness onset occurred between 6 and 28 April 2026 and was characterized by fever, gastrointestinal symptoms, rapid progression to pneumon

items.title: New Mexico — 2025 HPS, 7 confirmed (NM DOH)
items.summary: Seven confirmed HPS cases across Santa Fe, Taos, McKinley, and Bernalillo counties (the Bernalillo case was travel-associated). One reported death (Santa Fe County, 2025-03-07). State-DOH figure; the federal NNDSS aggregate currently shows 2 for the same state-year (state DOH and NNDSS use different update cadences and case definitions).
